doubtful_osteoarthritis: Shows only questionable or minimal radiographic changes, making it difficult to confidently diagnose osteoarthritis.
Unlike minimal or moderate osteoarthritis, there are no clear osteophytes or joint space narrowing.
Distinguished from healthy knees by the presence of subtle, non-specific irregularities.
Lacks the definitive features seen in more advanced osteoarthritis, such as pronounced bone spurs.
The changes are so slight that they may overlap with normal anatomical variations.
healthy_knee: No evidence of joint space narrowing, osteophyte formation, or bone sclerosis, unlike all osteoarthritis categories.
Easily distinguished from any osteoarthritic knee by the absence of degenerative changes.
The joint surfaces are smooth and regular, with no pathological findings.
Healthy knees do not show any of the subtle or obvious features present in diseased states.
Represents the baseline for comparison with all abnormal knee X-rays.
minimal_osteoarthritis: Shows early and mild features of osteoarthritis, such as tiny osteophytes or slight joint space narrowing.
Unlike doubtful osteoarthritis, the changes are definite but still very limited in extent.
Distinguished from moderate osteoarthritis by the lack of significant cartilage loss or bone sclerosis.
Minimal osteoarthritis does not yet cause major joint deformity or functional impairment.
The radiographic findings are more pronounced than doubtful, but less severe than moderate or advanced cases.
moderate_osteoarthritis: Exhibits clear joint space narrowing and moderate osteophyte formation, more advanced than minimal osteoarthritis.
Unlike severe osteoarthritis, the joint space is not completely lost and bone deformity is less pronounced.
Moderate osteoarthritis is characterized by visible sclerosis and more obvious degenerative changes.
The features are distinct from both early and late stages, representing an intermediate severity.
There is a balance of cartilage loss and bone changes, setting it apart from other categories.
severe_osteoarthritis: Marked by almost complete loss of joint space, large osteophytes, and significant bone deformity.
Easily distinguished from all other categories by the extent of structural damage and advanced degeneration.
Severe osteoarthritis shows bone-on-bone contact, which is not seen in milder forms.
The radiographic changes are extensive, with gross joint deformity and sclerosis.
This category represents the end stage of osteoarthritis, with features far beyond those of moderate disease.
